title: ulmus davidiana var. levigata
intro: Ulmus davidiana var. levigata, also known as the David Korean Elm or Levigate Elm, is a deciduous tree native to Korea and China, characterized by its distinctive bark and small leaves. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, making it useful in treating various health issues such as fever, skin problems, and respiratory conditions. The health benefits of Ulmus davidiana var. levigata include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and boosting the immune system, while its herbal preparations include teas, infusions, and tinctures that can be used to make extracts and ointments. Traditionally, the plant's bark and leaves are used in Korean medicine to treat a range of ailments, and it is also used in modern herbal medicine to support overall well-being.
